title: Smithsonia viridiflora
taxonomy: Smithsonia viridiflora is a species within the plant family Gesneriaceae. This family consists of flowering plants commonly known as the "African violet" or "Begonia" family, depending on the source. Smithsonia viridiflora is native to tropical Africa and is often classified as a type of epiphyte. Epiphytes are non-parasitic plants that grow on other plants for support. Smithsonia viridiflora is known for its unique flower color and growth habits.
uses: Smithsonia viridiflora is a plant species native to Australia, but information on its medicinal uses is limited.
